stories.story: In a scene worthy of any top cop show on TV, FBI agents and task force officers followed 36-year-old Kevone Charleston of Austell, Georgia, to a CVS pharmacy in Forsyth County, Georgia, early Saturday. FBI Special Agent Stephen Emmett said the incident happened around 7 o'clock Saturday morning. 36-year-old Kevone Charleston of Austell, Georgia is suspected of involvement in 32 commercial robberies dating to November 2013. After 36-year-old Kevone Charleston of Austell, Georgia parked and popped his hood, FBI agents and task force officers moved in as he walked to the CVS. When confronted, 36-year-old Kevone Charleston of Austell, Georgia ran to his car and traveled about 75 yards as FBI agents opened fire.
key_facts: 36-year-old Kevone Charleston of Austell, Georgia was followed to a CVS in Forsyth County, Georgia
Incident occurred around 7 o'clock Saturday morning
Kevone Charleston is suspected of 32 commercial robberies since November 2013
Kevone Charleston fled in his car 75 yards before FBI agents opened fire

stories.story: As 36-year-old Kevone Charleston of Austell, Georgia tried to flee, he rammed several FBI vehicles. Twelve FBI agents and six government vehicles followed 36-year-old Kevone Charleston of Austell, Georgia. 36-year-old Kevone Charleston of Austell, Georgia collided with a Taurus, a second vehicle, and an SUV that ended up on its side. One government vehicle sustained heavy damage to its front and side. Two FBI agents sustained injuries in these crashes.
key_facts: 12 FBI agents and 6 government vehicles were involved in the pursuit
Kevone Charleston rammed several vehicles, including a Taurus and an SUV
One government vehicle had heavy front and side damage
Two FBI agents were injured in the crashes
explicit_unknowns: The specific identities of the two injured FBI agents

stories.story: 36-year-old Kevone Charleston of Austell, Georgia was stopped 8 feet away in the median, where he was shot and wounded by FBI agents and task force officers. Forsyth County Sheriff's Deputy Robin Regan said 36-year-old Kevone Charleston of Austell, Georgia's injuries are not life threatening. FBI Special Agent Stephen Emmett said the two injured FBI agents were treated at an area hospital, released, and are fine. The FBI's violent crimes and major offender squad conducted an intensive investigation into 36-year-old Kevone Charleston of Austell, Georgia's 32 previous robberies. FBI Special Agent Stephen Emmett noted the investigation concluded and that 36-year-old Kevone Charleston of Austell, Georgia is in custody.
key_facts: Kevone Charleston was shot 8 feet away in the median
Forsyth County Sheriff's Deputy Robin Regan confirmed injuries were not life threatening
Two injured FBI agents were treated and released from a hospital
The FBI's violent crimes and major offender squad led the investigation

stories.story: FBI Special Agent Stephen Emmett said FBI agents and task force officers were prepared for an armed confrontation because 36-year-old Kevone Charleston of Austell, Georgia's MO involved armed confrontations. FBI Special Agent Stephen Emmett expressed relief that the FBI's officers and agents are OK. This event marked the conclusion of an intensive and lengthy investigation into 36-year-old Kevone Charleston of Austell, Georgia. CNN's Vivian Kuo and CNN's Ryan Scallan contributed to the report.
key_facts: Kevone Charleston's MO involved armed confrontations
FBI Special Agent Stephen Emmett confirmed the investigation has concluded
CNN's Vivian Kuo and Ryan Scallan contributed to the report
explicit_unknowns: Specifics of the armed confrontations in the suspect's MO
